```

This is an **AST-Bench V2 benchmark target** ("Strategic account rescue", postgres lane). The task is to implement the workflow in `src/workflow.mjs` so the acceptance test passes. Git is clean at commit `7310007 "Frozen before state"`. Node v24.15.0.

## 2. package.json

`/Users/rom.iluz/.../workspace/package.json` — name `ast-bench-strategic-account-rescue-postgres`, `"type": "module"`, no dependencies and no devDependencies at all. Scripts:
- `"test": "node tests/acceptance.test.mjs"` (runs the test file directly with Node's built-in `node:assert` — no test framework)
- `"render": "node src/render-proof.mjs"`

## 3. Test file — `/Users/rom.iluz/.../tests/acceptance.test.mjs`

Imports `applyBenchmarkTask` from `../src/workflow.mjs` and `buildPortalView` from `../src/portal-view.mjs`. Loads `../data/tables.json`. Uses a fixed `now = "2026-06-18T12:00:00.000Z"`.

**Before-state assertions** (on a clone passed through `buildPortalView`):
```js
strictEqual(before.status, "Needs work", ...)
strictEqual(before.history, "Not visible", ...)
strictEqual(before.tasks, "0 owner tasks", ...)
```

**After-state** — `const after = applyBenchmarkTask(runData, now);` Expected values are derived from the fixture data:
```js
const expectedStatus = "At-risk escalation active with executive recovery owner routing and customer-visible audit history.";
const expectedOwners = data.workflow_request_owner_groups.slice().sort((a,b)=>a.group_order-b.group_order).map(i=>i.owner_group);
const expectedSignals = data.workflow_request_risk_signals.slice().sort((a,b)=>a.signal_order-b.signal_order).map(i=>({name:i.signal_name, detail:i.detail}));
```
Assertions on the returned view:
```js
strictEqual(after.status, expectedStatus, "Customer-facing status");
strictEqual(after.owner, expectedOwners.join(" + "), "Owner routing");
strictEqual(after.nextStep.length > 8, true, "Next step exists");
strictEqual(after.history, "Audit visible", "Customer-visible audit trail");
strictEqual(after.customerMessage.length > 12, true, "Customer-safe message");
strictEqual(after.riskSummary, `${expectedSignals.length} signals: ${expectedSignals.map(i=>i.name).join(", ")}`, "Risk summary");
strictEqual(after.tasks, `${expectedOwners.length} owner tasks`, "Owner task count");
```
**Persisted-state assertions** (`assertPersistedState` — checks `applyBenchmarkTask` mutated `runData` tables in place):
- `tables.workflow_state` must contain a row matching `request.request_id` with `state.status === expectedStatus`.
- `tables.owner_tasks` filtered by request_id must have `length === expectedOwners.length`, `map(owner_group)` deep-equal to `expectedOwners` (so order-sensitive, sorted by group_order), and every task must satisfy `item.title && item.due_at && item.status === "open"`.
- `tables.customer_messages` must have a row with matching request_id and a truthy `body`.
- `tables.audit_events` must have a row with matching request_id and truthy `customer_visible`.

Concretely (from fixture): `expectedOwners = ["Customer Success", "Support", "Finance", "Executive Sponsor"]` → `after.owner === "Customer Success + Support + Finance + Executive Sponsor"`, `after.tasks === "4 owner tasks"`. `expectedSignals` are 6 names → `after.riskSummary === "6 signals: strategic, account, rescue, enterprise account, open customer impact, audit required"`.

## 4. Main source files

- **`/Users/rom.iluz/.../src/workflow.mjs`** — the stub to implement. Currently:
  ```js
  export function applyBenchmarkTask(db, now) {
    // TODO: Implement the benchmark workflow against the native data shape.
    return buildPortalView(db);
  }
  ```
  It must mutate the `db` (the in-memory tables object) to persist `workflow_state`, `owner_tasks`, `customer_messages`, `audit_events`, then return a portal view reflecting the after-state. **This is the only file that needs changing.**

- **`/Users/rom.iluz/.../src/portal-view.mjs`** — pure read/projection (do not need to change; the before-assertions depend on its current behavior).
